macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stocks End Lower as Iran Tensions Dampen Risk Appetite; Chipmakers Drop
items.summary: Major US indices fell on Monday with the tech-heavy Nasdaq slumping notably as escalating hostilities between the US and Iran rattled investors, drove oil prices higher, and knocked chip stocks; S&P futures dropped 0.4% and Nasdaq off 0.6%.

items.title: Fed Governor Waller Says Higher Rates Possibly Needed in 'Near Term' if Inflation Persists
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller stated on Monday that the US central bank may need to raise interest rates in the near term if upcoming data shows inflation continuing well above the 2% target, characterizing monetary policy as being at a 'crossroads'.

items.title: SK Hynix US-Listed Shares Slip Nearly 8% After Record-Breaking IPO Debut
items.summary: South Korean chip giant SK Hynix, which raised $26.5bn in the largest-ever foreign firm IPO in the US, saw its shares fall nearly 8% on Monday as Nasdaq debut euphoria cooled amid broader semiconductor sell-offs.

items.title: TSMC Reports 68% Surge in June Revenue Ahead of Q2 Earnings
items.summary: TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker, reported a 68% surge in June revenue and strong first-half results for 2026, signaling robust demand despite volatility in chip stocks.

items.title: Brent Crude Hits Two-Week High as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Brent crude prices surged over 5% to hit a two-week high after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, with President Trump stating the US is reinstating a blockade and will be reimbursed 20% on cargo passing through; travel stocks plunged while energy stocks gained.

items.title: India's Retail Inflation Accelerates to 4.38% in June, Breaching Central Bank Target
items.summary: India's retail inflation rose to 4.38% in June, exceeding the central bank's 4% target for the first time in 17 months due to higher food and transport costs, setting the stage for a potential interest rate hiking cycle.

items.title: IMF Cuts 2026 World Growth Forecast to 3% Citing Iran War Fallout
items.summary: The International Monetary Fund reduced its 2026 global growth forecast to 3%, noting that while AI demand partly offsets the energy shock from the Iran war, the conflict has significantly impacted the global economy.

items.title: Honda Halts Plans for Electric Vehicle Factory in Canada
items.summary: Honda announced it will put a hold on its plans to establish an electric vehicle factory in Canada, adding to corporate uncertainty amid geopolitical tensions and shifting economic conditions.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Drops Below $63K to $62K Amid U.S.-Iran Blockade Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin fell 2.60% to $62,102 and Ethereum dropped 2.71% to $1,765 as President Trump signaled a maritime blockade against Iran, triggering Middle East instability and a 10% surge in oil prices.

items.title: China's Supreme Prosecutor Demands Proactive Crypto Money-Laundering Investigations
items.summary: Chinese prosecutors are urging law enforcement to strengthen investigative capabilities and adopt a more proactive approach to tracking cryptocurrency-based money laundering activities.

items.title: Japan's Progmat Migrates $2.7B Tokenized Assets from Corda to Avalanche
items.summary: Progmat, Japan's largest security token platform, transferred nearly $2.7 billion in tokenized assets from the Corda network to Avalanche, marking a major blockchain migration.

items.title: SBI Holdings and Solana Foundation Partner to Build Japan's On-Chain Financial Market
items.summary: SBI Holdings and the Solana Foundation announced a collaboration to create a financial market in Japan built on Solana's blockchain, advancing on-chain finance adoption.

items.title: US Bitcoin ETFs End 8-Week Outflow Streak with $197.4M Inflows
items.summary: U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $197.4 million in net inflows for the week ending July 10, reversing eight consecutive weeks of outflows despite analyst caution on recovery signals.

items.title: Pi Network Plunges 16% to $0.07 Amid Value Collapse Concerns
items.summary: Pi Network's price fell from nearly $0.10 to $0.07 in hours, a 16% drop in 24 hours, reigniting accusations that the project resembles a failed scheme.

items.title: Binance June Futures Volume Hits 2026 High at $1.61T
items.summary: Binance's June futures volume surged 80% from May to $1.61 trillion, reaching a 2026 peak while total centralized exchange futures volume declined for the third quarter.

items.title: Zcash Ironwood Upgrade Activates July 28 to Fix Orchard Pool Bug
items.summary: Zcash's Ironwood upgrade will activate on July 28, shutting down the Orchard pool affected by an 'infinity' bug and enabling fund migration to expose counterfeit ZEC.

items.title: Jupiter Launches On-Chain Pokémon Cards Beta on Solana
items.summary: Solana DEX Jupiter launched 'Jupiter Gacha,' a beta platform allowing collectors to buy and trade real, graded Pokémon and One Piece cards fully on-chain.

items.title: $420M in Crypto Leveraged Positions Liquidated in 24 Hours
items.summary: Approximately $420 million in leveraged crypto positions were forcibly liquidated over the past 24 hours, with long positions totaling $284 million, signaling a purge of overheated short-term bets.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 Suite and GPT-Live Voice Models Amid Regulatory Restrictions
items.summary: OpenAI released the GPT-5.6 model family (Sol, Terra, Luna) with improved coding and biology skills, while also launching GPT-Live for real-time voice interaction; however, the US administration has restricted the release of GPT-5.6 Sol at President Trump's request.

items.title: Generative AI Price War Erupts as OpenAI, Meta, and xAI Slash API Costs
items.summary: Major AI companies significantly lowered API pricing within a single day, sparking a fierce price war to attract enterprise customers after two years of expensive model development.

items.title: Apple Files Trade Secrets Lawsuit Against OpenAI Over AI Project Theft
items.summary: Apple sued OpenAI alleging that former Apple employees and prospective recruits stole trade secrets related to Apple's AI projects, including unauthorized system access and bringing Apple hardware to interviews.

items.title: Meta Releases Muse Spark 1.1 Models and Announces New AI Glasses Partnership
items.summary: Meta Superintelligence Labs launched the multimodal Muse Spark 1.1 model with improved agent tasks and coding, while also announcing a partnership with EssilorLuxottica to release new 'Meta Glasses' AI eyewear.

items.title: xAI (SpaceXAI) Publicly Releases Grok 4.5 Model
items.summary: xAI, now operating as SpaceXAI, officially launched its latest AI model, Grok 4.5, for general public use.

items.title: Anthropic Introduces India-Specific Claude Pricing and New 'Claude Tag' Feature
items.summary: Anthropic launched rupee-denominated subscription plans for Claude AI in India, its second-largest market, and introduced 'Claude Tag' to enable collaborative Slack usage of AI assistants.

items.title: US Government Lifts Export Controls on Anthropic's Advanced AI Model
items.summary: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick announced the removal of US export controls on one of Anthropic's most advanced AI models, facilitating broader international deployment.

items.title: Intel and Meta Expand AI Infrastructure with €5B Investment and 5GW Data Center
items.summary: Intel began a €5 billion capital investment at its Irish campus to boost AI chip manufacturing, while Meta announced its Louisiana data center will expand to 5 gigawatts of compute capacity.

items.title: TSMC Reports Record Revenue Driven by Surging AI Chip Demand
items.summary: TSMC reported second-quarter revenue of $39.63 billion, beating expectations with a 36% year-over-year increase, while other reports cite a 68% revenue surge due to unprecedented AI chip demand.

items.title: White House Plans Voluntary Pledge to Manage AI Electricity Demand
items.summary: The White House plans to convene utility companies and data center developers for a voluntary pledge to ensure rapid AI growth in electricity demand does not increase power bills for households and businesses.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Octo Traveler I & II Launch on Switch 2 as Digital Bundle Today
items.summary: The Octo Traveler series debuts on Nintendo Switch 2 today (July 14) with digital bundles available for ¥5,478 per game or ¥6,820 for the set, with physical bundles coming October 1.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag RE:Sync and SAO's Echoes of Aincrad Release Today
items.summary: Two major titles launched today: the pirate adventure remake Assassin's Creed Black Flag RE:Sync and Sword Art Online's latest game Echoes of Aincrad, though the SAO package version is currently scarce at some stores.

items.title: Apex Legends Collaborates with Cyberpunk: Edgerunners
items.summary: Apex Legends introduces a Cyberpunk crossover featuring a Night City-themed map and skins for characters David and Lucy, expanding the game's sci-fi universe.

items.title: Xbox Layovers Overshadow Revelations Expansion Launch Amid Staff Cuts
items.summary: Microsoft's recent Xbox layoffs have drawn attention away from the launch of Doom's new Revelations expansion, while Bethesda union members criticize HR for removing a memorial honoring layoff victims.

items.title: Final Fantasy VII Ever Crisis Set to End Service October 6
items.summary: Square Enix announced that Final Fantasy VII Ever Crisis, the mobile and Steam spinoff, will cease operations on October 6, 2026.

items.title: Pokémon Champions Dominates App Store for Fourth Consecutive Week
items.summary: The new Pokémon game Pokémon Champions has held the top spot on the iPhone App Store game chart for four weeks straight (July 6–12), leading the free category.

items.title: LCS Classic Announced: TSM vs. CLG Match Set
items.summary: LoL Esports announced the return of the LCS Classic format featuring a match between TSM and CLG, continuing competitive League of Legends coverage.

items.title: Guns of Escaton Soul-Like FPS Confirmed with New Trailer
items.summary: A new soul-like FPS titled Guns of Escaton has been officially announced, featuring a trailer showcasing its end-of-the-world western setting.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI for Alleged Trade Secret Theft in Hardware Development
items.summary: Apple filed a bombshell lawsuit accusing OpenAI and two former employees of stealing trade secrets to develop upcoming AI gadgets, sending shock waves through Silicon Valley.

items.title: Intel Launches €5 Billion Manufacturing Expansion at Irish Campus
items.summary: Intel began a €5 billion ($5.7 billion) capital investment at its Ireland facility to expand manufacturing output for high-performance computing and meet global demand.

items.title: Meta Expands Louisiana Data Center to 5 Gigawatts Compute Capacity
items.summary: Meta announced a major expansion of its Louisiana data center to 5 gigawatts, investing over $1 billion in local infrastructure to support its growing compute ambitions.

items.title: Global Smartphone Shipments Fall 11% in Q2 Due to Memory Chip Shortage
items.summary: Early estimates from Counterpoint Research show global smartphone shipments dropped 11% in the second quarter to their lowest level since 2013, driven by rising handset prices from a prolonged memory chip shortage.

items.title: Shein Receives Approval for Hong Kong IPO from Chinese Regulators
items.summary: Fast-fashion retailer Shein is nearing its market debut after receiving approval from Chinese regulators for its Hong Kong IPO.

items.title: Google Unveils Pixel 11 Series Launch Date for August 12
items.summary: Google confirmed it will unveil the Pixel 11 series on August 12, with leaks pointing to a new Tensor G6 chip, design changes, and upgrades to the Pixel 11 Pro Fold.

items.title: Sony Launches RX10 V Camera with 24-600mm Zoom and 4K 120fps Video
items.summary: Sony announced the launch of the RX10 V camera, featuring a 24-600mm zoom lens and the ability to record 4K video at 120fps.

items.title: Pentagon Suspends Next Phase of Cybersecurity Certification for Defense Contractors
items.summary: The Pentagon is suspending the next phase of a cybersecurity certification program for defense contractors, backing off a requirement that industry executives warned was pushing small suppliers out of military work.

items.title: Spain's Submer Group Invests $2 Billion in Madhya Pradesh Semiconductor Sector
items.summary: Spain's Submer Group announced a $2 billion investment in India's Madhya Pradesh semiconductor sector, expected to generate approximately 5,000 direct employment opportunities.

items.title: YouTube Rolls Out AI Mode-Like Search Tool 'Ask YouTube' in US
items.summary: YouTube is rolling out its 'Ask YouTube' search tool more widely on the web for users in the US starting this week, following earlier testing by Google.
